mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance against rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, causing corporate earnings revisions to turn negative and driving S&P 500 forward returns down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation signals robust economic demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and stable CPIAUCSL yields a negative rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect immediate capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance against INDPRO month-over-month changes over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payroll employment directly increases household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.0 percent indicates a sudden consumer demand shock that forces immediate earnings downgrades, driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of instances following a >1.0% retail sales decline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index signal aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and reduces forward S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL yields positive forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST series shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a rolling rank IC consistently below 0.01.
